source: Claims against Malayan Government. James Miller & Co.__CO273:670:6
filename: Claims against Malayan Government. James Miller & Co.__CO273:670:6.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** British Colonial Office/Dominions Office handling of **claims against the Malayan (and Straits Settlements) Government(s)**, chiefly the claim by **Messrs. James Miller & Co.** for unpaid pre-occupation supplies to the **Malayan Veterinary Department**; file also references **Singapore Cold Storage Co. Ltd.** and other claims.  
- **Geography & time period:** Concerns **Malaya/Singapore** (incl. Kuala Lumpur, Johore Bahru, Penang; Brinchang mentioned in a related claim) with processing and correspondence in **London, Canberra, and Sydney**; main dates **1942–1943** (with supplies/events in **1941–Feb 1942** before/at the Japanese occupation).  
- **Major decision/event (Miller claim):** After initial inclination to **defer/“register”** the claim due to lack of proof (e.g., telegram/draft decisions in **May–June 1943**), the **Secretary of State for the Colonies** later **authorised settlement** in **Sept 1943** subject to safeguards.  
- **Claim details & evidence:** Miller’s claim totals **Straits $23,670** for three invoices: **Bill 343 $4,720** (disc harrow cultivators; ordered July 1941; arrived Nov 1941; distributed in Malaya), **Bill 356 $16,750** (Caterpillar D.6 tractor delivered Johore Nov 1941; used near Johore Bahru), **Bill 400 $2,200** (mowing machines arrived Jan 1942; left in Singapore Harbour Board godown; “landed but not delivered”). Evidence includes a **12 Feb 1942 cable** seeking payment, **H.M. Trade Commissioner Sydney certificate (7 Jul 1943)** citing debtor lists showing **$21,470** outstanding (excluding Bill 400), and a corroborating letter from **Capt. A.P. Helm** (US Army Engineers, New Guinea) dated **14 Jul 1943**.  
- **Settlement conditions/financial terms:** Payment in Australia of **$23,670** was authorised (noted in despatch **No. 190, 13 Sep 1943**) at exchange **$1 = 2/4d**, **only upon** a **letter of indemnity** backed by **bank guarantee/sureties**, with an undertaking to **refund** any overpayment later proved; accounting guidance suggests charging to **“Malaya (Unallocated) Account.”**  
- **Key actors/organisations:** **Colonial Office** (incl. J. J. Paskin/Parkin; officials Gent, Clauson, Jeffries, etc.), **Dominions Office** (incl. C.R. Attlee as Secretary of State for Dominion Affairs; M.E. Antrobus), **High Commissioner for the UK in Australia (Canberra)**, **Crown Agents (Pay Department)**, **Trading with the Enemy Department** (D. Carter), **Malayan Government Agent, Sydney**, **H.M. Senior Trade Commissioner, Sydney**, and Malayan officials **Capt. S.H. Whitworth** (Veterinary Adviser/Director; reported interned) and **William Orr** (Veterinary Officer, Singapore).  
- **Related/parallel matters in file:** Discussion of **Trading with the Enemy Act 1939** issues (whether a company is “enemy,” effect of licences/authorities, and risks of obtaining a “good discharge”); other claims referenced include **C.H. Young & Co.** (**S.S. $3,500** for sanitary installation at Brinchang and fittings, requiring T/E Dept authority and indemnity), **Singapore Cold Storage Co. Ltd.** (claim treated as registration case due to inability to confirm local settlement), and **Kinloch & Co.** claim **$62,745** (Penang works contract) mentioned in an **11 Apr 1942** telegram.
